metadata.title: Owl tracks in the meadow snow
metadata.curiosityNote: Great gray owls hunt by sound and plunge through snow to reach voles below.

story.briefTemplate: At the edge of a frozen Minnesota meadow, Seraphina and Grandma found {quantity:morning_tracks} in the snow. A gust of powdery wind swept over the trail and buried {quantity:buried_tracks}. Then a great gray owl dropped into the snow after a hidden vole and left {quantity:owl_wing_marks} nearby.
story.causalEvent: A gust of powdery snow buried part of the trail.
story.closingNoteTemplate: The field log closed with {quantity:total_wildlife_tracks} counted at the meadow's edge.

label.child: Animal tracks found in the morning
Tracks still visible after the gust
Wing marks the owl left in the snow
Wildlife tracks in the field log

steps.prompt: How many tracks were still visible after the gust?
steps.reasoningPrompt: After the snow covered part of the trail, did the visible track count go up or down?

backwardCheck.prompt: Add the buried tracks back. Do you land on the morning count?

steps.prompt: How many total wildlife tracks remained?
steps.reasoningPrompt: The visible trail tracks and the owl wing marks are both in the field log. How do they relate to the total?

backwardCheck.prompt: Take the owl marks back out. Do you reach the visible trail tracks?

operatorExperiments.alternateWorldTemplate: Adding would fit a different story where {quantity:buried_tracks} of fresh prints arrived instead of being buried.
This matches the story: the gust buried {quantity:buried_tracks}, so those marks leave the trail.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:buried_tracks} patches like {label:morning_tracks.lowercase}, how many would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:morning_tracks.lowercase} into equal groups.
This matches the story: {label:visible_tracks.lowercase} and {label:owl_wing_marks.lowercase} join in the field log.
Subtracting would fit a different story where {quantity:owl_wing_marks} were brushed away instead of added.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:owl_wing_marks} groups like {label:visible_tracks.lowercase}, how many would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:visible_tracks.lowercase} into equal groups.

recap.headline: Why the field log ended with {quantity:total_wildlife_tracks}
causalChain: The owl added {quantity:owl_wing_marks}, making {quantity:total_wildlife_tracks} in the field log.

dataQuestion.prompt: What does {quantity:buried_tracks} represent in the first model?
dataQuestion.correctFeedback: Right. {quantity:buried_tracks} is the part of the trail the snow covered.
dataQuestion.incorrectFeedback: That amount is the covered part of the trail: {quantity:buried_tracks}.
